segments.text: Now Pashhur the son of Immer, the priest who was also chief governor in the house of the Lord, heard that Jeremiah prophesied these things.
Then Pashhur struck Jeremiah the prophet, and put him in the stocks that were in the high gate of Benjamin, which was by the house of the Lord.
And it happened on the next day that Pashhur brought Jeremiah out of the stocks. Then Jeremiah said to him,
The Lord has not called your name Pashhur, but Magor-Missabib.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: For thus says the Lord:

Behold, I will make you a terror to yourself and to all your friends; and they shall fall by the sword of their enemies, and your eyes shall see it. I will give all Judah into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall carry them captive to Babylon and slay them with the sword.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Moreover I will deliver all the wealth of this city, all its produce, and all its precious things; all the treasures of the kings of Judah I will give into the hand of their enemies, who will plunder them, seize them, and carry them to Babylon.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And you, Pashhur, and all who dwell in your house, shall go into captivity. You shall go to Babylon, and there you shall die, and be buried there, you and all your friends, to whom you have prophesied lies.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: O Lord, You induced me, and I was persuaded; You are stronger than I, and have prevailed. I am in derision daily; Everyone mocks me.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: For when I spoke, I cried out; I shouted, 'Violence and plunder!' Because the word of the Lord was made to me A reproach and a derision daily.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Then I said, 'I will not make mention of Him, Nor speak anymore in His name.' But His word was in my heart like a burning fire Shut up in my bones; I was weary of holding it back, And I could not.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: For I heard many mocking:
Fear on every side!
segments.speaker: Mockers

segments.text: Report, and we will report it!
segments.speaker: Mockers

segments.text: All my acquaintances watched for my stumbling, saying,
Perhaps he can be induced; Then we will prevail against him, And we will take our revenge on him.
segments.speaker: Acquaintances

segments.text: But the Lord is with me as a mighty, awesome One. Therefore my persecutors will stumble, and will not prevail. They will be greatly ashamed, for they will not prosper. Their everlasting confusion will never be forgotten.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: But, O Lord of hosts, You who test the righteous, And see the mind and heart, Let me see Your vengeance on them; For I have pleaded my cause before You.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Sing to the Lord! Praise the Lord! For He has delivered the life of the poor From the hand of evildoers.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Cursed be the day in which I was born! Let the day not be blessed in which my mother bore me!
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Let the man be cursed Who brought news to my father, saying, 'A male child has been born to you!' Making him very glad.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: And let that man be like the cities Which the Lord overthrew, and did not relent; Let him hear the cry in the morning And the shouting at noon,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Because he did not kill me from the womb, That my mother might have been my grave, And her womb always enlarged with me.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Why did I come forth from the womb to see labor and sorrow, That my days should be consumed with shame?
segments.speaker: Jeremiah
